methods.description: Process for measuring the depth of ice cores to ensure all analyses are on the same reference depth scale, addressing issues such as broken cores and depth offsets.

steps.description: Measure the depth of an ice core from the logging of the ice cores.
Correct for depth offsets due to core inclination, expansion, temperature differences, and gauge calibration.
steps.searchable_summary: correct depth offsets in logged data

methods.description: Procedure for collecting and processing ECM data from ice cores to analyze electrical conductivity, ensuring data consistency across different field seasons.

steps.description: Preheat ice sections overnight at −15°C and cut a 1 cm slice using a horizontal saw.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare ice sections for ECM measurement

steps.description: Perform ECM measurements using electrodes with a 1500 V potential difference, recording the signal.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct ECM measurements on ice slices

steps.description: Remove aberrant events corresponding to ice core breaks and resample data every centimeter.
Transfer depths from VK-BH8 and VK-BH7 to VK-5G depth using common volcanic events.
steps.searchable_summary: align ECM depths using volcanic events

methods.description: Method for measuring deuterium and oxygen-18 contents in ice cores to infer past temperature changes, ensuring precision and consistency across samples.

steps.description: Extract samples from a band along the ice cores for isotope measurement.
steps.searchable_summary: extract ice core samples for isotope analysis

steps.description: Conduct isotope measurements using the uranium reduction technique with automatic injection device.
steps.searchable_summary: perform isotope measurements using uranium reduction

steps.description: Follow protocol of four injections per water sample with at least two independent measurements for precision.
